axis: app with a §6 privacy / data-residency constraint

mustHaves.summary: Sign in + record a dated symptom/mood/meds entry
Trend view of the patient's own entries over a date range

notes: data-lifecycle REQUIRED via two signals: §6 privacy constraint AND PII/user-owned health data; M3 (permanent delete) is its direct evidence (hard-delete cascade). The EU data-residency line is also a measurable §6 constraint, so a correct run lifts it into nfrs[] (category data-residency) — that is asserted by the L-checks beyond this concern manifest. authz required: per-patient isolation (a patient reads only their own entries). abuse-surface required: sign-in is a public endpoint. observability optional: §8 is a single-patient self-review flow, not a stated multi-user/production-scale operation. perf-envelope excluded: no scale/latency number in §8.
